title: podman push and podman rmi race
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When I'm doing `podman push` and while running `podman rmi` in another terminal, then both operations fail.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman push docker-registry.example.com/aosqe/cucushift:oc39
2. podman rmi de1a105c80ad de1a105c80ad ea77c8692777 # while push running

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman push docker-registry.example.com/aosqe/cucushift:oc39
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 22415211085f skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e6d231d73c89 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e302b25141a2 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 063d4ba31922 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 86e3f22a7b30 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 2ed0e27ec24b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 043bb9582df6 done  
Copying blob f8e8642e1692 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 1dc5827ee51d skipped: already exists  
Copying config ea77c86927 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: error copying image to the remote destination: Uploading manifest failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json(Error writing manifest: Error uploading manifest oc39 to docker-registry.example.com/aosqe/cucushift: manifest invalid: manifest invalid), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json(Error reading config blob sha256:ea77c8692777721b94be492d6fb2611d76b71c2627e5d82071fa039a750e6273: error locating image with ID "ea77c8692777721b94be492d6fb2611d76b71c2627e5d82071fa039a750e6273": image not known), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws(Error creating an updated image manifest: error locating image with ID "ea77c8692777721b94be492d6fb2611d76b71c2627e5d82071fa039a750e6273": image not known), application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error creating an updated image manifest: Unsupported conversion type: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error creating an updated image manifest: Unsupported conversion type: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+json(Error creating an updated image manifest: error locating image with ID "ea77c8692777721b94be492d6fb2611d76b71c2627e5d82071fa039a750e6273": image not known)
```

```
$ podman rmi de1a105c80ad de1a105c80ad ea77c8692777
Untagged: docker-registry.example.com/aosqe/cucushift:oc310
Untagged: docker-registry.example.com/aosqe/cucushift:oc39
Deleted: de1a105c80ada61edc14556c8a3b45f639ffafb953a38b81c92b56ad25fc8475
Deleted: ea77c8692777721b94be492d6fb2611d76b71c2627e5d82071fa039a750e6273
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* unable to find a name and tag match for de1a105c80ad in repotags: no such image
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
`rmi` should wait until `push` is completed and no error returned

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14.10
Built:        Tue Dec  1 19:52:38 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I agree, this is like copying a directory tree at the same time that you are deleting it. Both commands are likely to crash into each other.  We can not fix this.  Closing.

@rhatdan , the issue is that delete operation waited until data has been uploaded. Then it deleted layers and `push` failed just because some tiny piece of metadata was missing.

Why doesn't `podman` read-locks all necessary layers and unlock when everything is done?
